title: States are the Solution, Not the Problem
author: Peter De Clercq
standfirst: Sustainable protection in displacement contexts must be anchored in national systems, local ownership, and the rule of law.

title: The System We Forgot We Were Building
author: Inah Kaloga
affiliation: Global Director, Violence Prevention & Response, IRC
standfirst: Humanitarian protection and social work have always been more closely connected than the sector often admits.

title: When Protection Leadership Is Not Enough
author: Samuel Cheung
standfirst: Protection outcomes often depend less on formal policy commitments than on leadership choices, institutional incentives, and mandate design.

title: Humanitarian policy reform cannot substitute for displacement governance.
author: Samuel Cheung
standfirst: After two decades of policy innovation, protection still often fails where institutional responsibility remains fragmented.

title: The Problem No One Owns
author: Samuel Cheung
standfirst: Internal displacement reveals what happens when protection needs are clear but responsibility remains fragmented.
